Canada enters as the model's favorite at 41%, holding a modest edge over Switzerland's 27% win probability. The 14-percentage-point gap reflects a slight but meaningful advantage in the statistical forecast. Switzerland's 27% chance nonetheless represents a credible path to victory in what the model assesses as a competitive fixture.

The draw carries material weight at 31%, suggesting neither side is strongly favored to settle the match decisively. The spread across win/draw/loss outcomes is relatively flat; Canada's lead in win probability is modest enough that a stalemate or a Swiss upset remain plausible outcomes. Competitive balance in this range typically signals a close contest that could unfold in multiple ways.

No sportsbook consensus is available yet. The model's probabilities will be updated as market odds emerge and the match approaches. The dashboard cannot assess divergence between statistical forecast and market pricing until bookmaker data becomes available.
